positioningFacts.rawValue: Wandercraft positions Calvin as a heavy-duty carrier robot that moves heavy loads, handles boxes and crates, and picks small parts with precision; it is described as fully autonomous and intended for factories and logistics hubs.
positioningFacts.reason: 今回、公式位置付けの記述を開いて再確認した。

positioningFacts.rawValue: The current Calvin page says it is designed for heavy loads and precision tasks, is deployed in actual production, and is the first humanoid deployed in Europe for assembly-line production tasks.
positioningFacts.reason: 今回、公式位置付けの記述を開いて再確認した。

positioningFacts.rawValue: Calvin is trained on 12 generic industrial/logistics use cases, three of which the official page says are already deployed; the page describes continuous operation from 8 to 22 hours per day, not a per-charge battery runtime.
positioningFacts.reason: 今回、公式位置付けの記述を開いて再確認した。

usageExamples.title: High torque. High reliability. Built for the industry.
usageExamples.summary: 現行公式製品ページがCalvinを実際の生産環境に配備済みと説明し、欧州の組立ライン生産タスクでの配備と3件の運用中ユースケースに言及。ただし顧客拠点・作業名・数量は同ページで非公開。

proposedValue.title: High torque. High reliability. Built for the industry.

conflicts: Current Aeolus product pages use the name aeo, while Japanese deployment releases from 2020 and 2022 use the generic name Aeolus Robot and do not specify a generation; deployment examples are linked but current-variant equivalence remains unconfirmed.
Repository contains a 150cm height and current Japan-support assertion without a current official aeo numeric/spec source; these values were not used as found evidence.
humanReviewRequired: Confirm whether the 2020/2022 Japanese deployment units are the current aeo variant or an earlier Aeolus Robot generation.
Confirm current aeo height, weight, speed, DoF, payload, battery and safety documentation with Aeolus/Marubun before public numeric display.
